project_overview.project_name: Ethiopia Education and Skills for Employability Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve employment outcomes of the TVET system of Ethiopia with a focus on women and marginalized groups
project_overview.beneficiaries: At least 200,000 youth (3% of NEET youth) directly through high quality training and linkages to employment, with at least 50% women, 15% IDPs, and 8% PWDs

primary_locations: National coverage with at least one PTC selected in each region
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Project specifically targets conflict-affected regions and IDPs. Ethiopia has experienced devastating internal conflict since November 2020, with 4.39 million IDPs and ongoing scattered conflicts in some regions

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Strengthening public polytechnics for improved labor market outcomes
key_components.description: Transform 24 selected PTCs into Centers of Excellence through Strategic Investment Plans, Industry Advisory Boards, new training programs, entrepreneurship training, trainer capacity building, and facility upgrades
Support short-term skills programs through competitive performance-based financing model linking payments to employment outcomes, targeting 100,000 youth with priority for women, IDPs, PWDs
Enhance information systems, coordination mechanisms, and capacity through SMIS, Sector Skills Councils establishment, and policy development support

implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee chaired by MoLS Minister, Delivery Unit within MoLS, Project Implementation Teams at each PTC and EDI, Regional Skills Bureaus coordination
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF, competitive selection for Component 2 training providers, performance-based contracting
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Delivery Unit responsible for overall monitoring, annual tracer studies, third-party verification, rigorous impact evaluation with randomized control trial for Component 2

identified_risks.risk_description: Complex conflict system with ethnic tensions, armed conflict since November 2020, social and political unrest, volatile security situation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project provides positive externalities by giving displaced populations skills for employment, increased access to training across country, detailed security risk assessments and management plans

identified_risks.risk_description: High external and domestic debt, severe foreign exchange shortages, rising inflation, declining tax to GDP ratio, impact of conflict on service delivery expenditures
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Promote partnership with enterprises for cooperative training, contract private training providers who are more cost effective

identified_risks.risk_description: New Ministry (MoLS) may lack capacity to implement reforms, introduction of several innovations including larger role for private sector, fragmentation in sector
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Technical support for new Ministry, involve private sector at various levels, entrench reforms in sector governance structures during implementation

identified_risks.risk_description: Delays in AWP&B, low budget utilizations, low staffing capacity, weak internal control over assets management, PTCs lacking World Bank project experience
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Comprehensive action plan including early AWP&B submission, dedicated project FM staff recruitment, capacity building trainings, separate manual for Component 2

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS2: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
ESS7: Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African Historically Underserved Traditional Local Communities
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Project-level grievance mechanisms established, World Bank Grievance Redress Service available, grievance redress committee to review and resolve citizen complaints
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Strong focus on women's participation with at least 50% female beneficiaries, interventions to encourage women in non-traditional courses, information sharing on profitability of male-dominated sectors, safe learning environments

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project specifically designed to address conflict impacts by targeting IDPs (at least 15% of beneficiaries), providing skills training for displaced populations to find gainful employment and stability, includes special window for IDPs in Component 2
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia marked as fragile state, experiencing devastating internal conflict since November 2020, 4.39 million IDPs, ongoing scattered conflicts, peace agreement reached November 2022 but volatility continues, influx of refugees from Sudan conflict
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Detailed security risk assessments and management plans prepared, security risks may hinder smooth implementation especially for PTCs in conflict regions, volatile security situation affects service delivery
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Project specifically targets 4.39 million IDPs from conflict, at least 15% of beneficiaries to be IDPs, special training programs for conflict-affected regions, influx of refugees from Sudan putting additional pressure
